what_it_does: Recovers standing rules, laws, constraints, and resource facts that bind how the recollection/episodic-memory system must be built, gathered before loop-prep so the build starts from already-decided material rather than fresh design.
observations: 38 constraints captured and grouped into North Star + Pillars, Build Process, Architecture, Model/Resource, Communication/Verification, and Identity frames.
Each entry includes the constraint, source note, evidence tag ([Observed] or [Inferred]), and a one-line 'what it means for recollection's build' translation.
Evidence tags distinguish directly-read rules from reasoning about how a rule applies to recollection.
The North Star is identity continuity / non-repetition; the two pillars are cross-project omniscience and a shared mechanism of distill + provenance + temporal/scale axes + pool-across-time.

Build-process rules include no-versioning, no branches in ~/company, no-MVP prioritization, no deferral, scaffolding ≠ spec, incomplete work in-scope, deliver whole slates, habitual delegation, loops as workflows + dialectic rounds, and embedding-work through memory first.

Architecture laws include no-hardcoding/registry-is-truth, grounded-chain law, extraction-vs-judgment, not-agent-architecture by default, MCP as top priority, no silent failures, not-a-replacement/bge-m3 zero priority, use existing resources, and code-as-lossy-projection.

Model/resource facts include ~15.5 GB GPU budget, consult Tim before loading models, mode→loadout registry, native model layer, on-disk model estate, don't use old episodic-memory plugin, and local stack ground facts.

Communication/verification rules include confirm before writing, honest status with ✅/🟡/🔴, verify before claiming, maximal capture, expand-don't-echo, make developer calls, reasoning in messages, dense-transmission protocol, make-each-thing-work, and render for Tim's cognition.
Identity/entity rules include Company-as-one-entity with memory as bloodstream, and autonomous spawn is lead-only.
A 'Count + Top-5' section summarizes the most build-critical constraints.
The file is explicitly a living doc updated in place per the no-versioning rule.
standouts: North Star: recollection exists for identity continuity / non-repetition, not transcript search.
Registry-is-truth / no-hardcoding is treated as the root failure mode for the whole system.
Extraction-vs-judgment mandates a two-stage architecture: small models emit observations, central judge reasons relationally.
bge-m3 has ZERO priority; the lens/loadout is an open, co-residence-budgeted registry, not a default.
Success is verify-by-use through the company MCP, not retrieval precision metrics.
conventions: Taxonomic grouping with letter-number identifiers (A1, B2, etc.).
Each constraint followed by a one-line build implication prefixed with '→'.
Source citations to memory notes and the living EPISODIC-UPGRADE-PLAN.md.
Self-referential update rule (no-versioning) applied to the file itself.
Neutral framing with no ranking language outside the author's own 'Top-5' list.
concerns: A large number of constraints (38) may be difficult to keep simultaneously salient during implementation.
Some entries mix observed rules with inferred application and leave open questions (e.g., central judge default model).
The 'Top-5' is itself a ranking, which the surrounding neutral framing says to avoid.
Overlap between constraints (e.g., half-the-power and use-existing-resources) is noted but could complicate traceability.
notes: A pre-build guardrail document that translates Tim's standing directives and resource facts into project-specific implications for the recollection system.
questions: How will each constraint be checked off or validated during the build?
What is the exact procedure for a 'mini loop-prep' when a gap is found in pre-built scaffolding?
How will the default central judge model be decided and recorded?
What is the relationship between this ledger and the loop-prep triad (Completion Criteria, Implementation Guide, Research Synthesis)?
How will the correction-propagation mechanism implied by the no-repeat KPI be designed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to compile binding constraints before loop-prep so the build starts from already-decided rules; the file does exactly that by listing, sourcing, tagging, and translating constraints into build implications.
apparent_themes: MCP as the primary agent-facing surface
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent repeated prior failures by making every binding rule visible and actionable before construction begins, and to keep the recollection build aligned with Tim's standing directives rather than letting design drift.
novelty: A constraints ledger format that pairs each rule with an evidence tag, source note, and a project-specific 'what it means' translation, plus an explicit inferred/observed distinction.
contribution: A structured, evidence-tagged inventory of binding build rules with per-rule implications, usable as a guardrail checklist and canonical reference during the recollection build.
summary_for_embedding: A wave-1 constraints ledger for the recollection episodic-memory build that compiles 38 sourced, evidence-tagged standing rules and resource facts into grouped build implications.
intention_for_embedding: To ensure the recollection build starts from already-decided constraints rather than fresh design, and to keep the build aligned with Tim's standing directives.
